— ˗ ˋ ୨୧ ˊ˗ — || oh i miss kpop 2023 era 🥹🥹 || #newjeans #luvqkyung #fyyp #2023kpop #xybca || Many K-pop fans miss 2023 because it felt like one of the last years where everything about K-pop seemed exciting, fresh, and emotionally connected at the same time. The music was everywhere, with groups like NewJeans, LE SSERAFIM, IVE, Stray Kids, SEVENTEEN, and TXT dominating playlists with songs that felt both nostalgic and modern. Fans remember how every comeback became a huge event online, when TikTok challenges, fancams, and award show performances brought everyone together in a way that felt fun instead of overly competitive. There was also a unique aesthetic to 2023 K-pop the Y2K fashion, dreamy summer concepts, emotional late-night songs, and colorful music videos created a comforting atmosphere that many people now associate with happier memories in their own lives. For international fans, it was also a time when K-pop felt more globally connected than ever, with world tours, festival appearances, and idols constantly interacting with fans through livestreams and social media. Looking back, people miss not just the music itself, but the feeling of excitement, community, and escape that 2023 K-pop gave them during that era || ❕❕

Minju is a South Korean singer and performer best known as a member of the K-pop girl group ILLIT. She gained attention for her calm charisma, expressive vocals, and elegant stage presence, helping the group stand out after their debut under [BELIFT LAB](https://beliftlab.com?utm_source=chatgpt.com).  Fans often praise Minju for her polished visuals and steady performances, as well as the warm and thoughtful personality she shows in interviews and behind-the-scenes content. Since ILLIT’s debut, she has become one of the group’s most recognizable members, contributing to their growing popularity in the global K-pop scene. #minju #illit #wl #wlmotivation

한 그릇만 주문해도, 커다란 양푼에서 바로 만들어주는 의정부 비빔국수 📍의정부제일시장 '영선네 잔치국수' (주소: 경기 의정부시 태평로73번길 20 서52호) 🔗네이버 지도 링크: https://naver.me/IGGYPWYs 🥢비빔국수 8천원 의정부 제일시장의 오랜 내공이 담긴 국수 맛집, '영선네'입니다. ​이곳의 인기 비결은 주문 즉시 커다란 양푼에서 사장님이 직접 손맛으로 무쳐주시는 비빔국수입니다. 한자리에서만 무려 20년 동안 변함없는 맛을 이어온 진정한 노포 국수 맛집으로, ​매일 아침부터 점심시간까지는 든든하게 한 끼 식사를 해결하러 오시는 손님들로 발 디딜 틈이 없습니다. ​아삭아삭한 식감의 콩나물이 듬뿍 올라간 비빔국수와 사장님이 직접 정성껏 담그신 열무김치의 조합은 그야말로 일품인데요🤤 입맛 당기는 매콤새콤한 비빔국수를 좋아하시는 분들이라면 꼭 한번 방문해 보세요! - Made in a Giant Mixing Bowl! The Legendary 20-Year Hand-Mixed Spicy Noodles 📍 Yeong-seonne Noodles, Uijeongbu Jeil Market (Address: 20 Taepyeong-ro 73beon-gil, Uijeongbu-si, Gyeonggi-do) 🔗 Map: https://naver.me/IGGYPWYs 🥢 Spicy Mixed Noodles (Bibim-guksu): 8,000 KRW Welcome to 'Yeong-seonne,' a noodle spot in Uijeongbu Jeil Market with years of expertise. The secret to its popularity is the spicy mixed noodles, hand-tossed by the owner in a giant mixing bowl (Yangpun) immediately after ordering. It’s a true hidden gem that has maintained the same delicious taste in the same spot for over 20 years. From early morning through lunch, it’s always packed with locals looking for a hearty meal. The combination of spicy noodles topped with crunchy bean sprouts and homemade young summer radish kimchi (Yeolmu-kimchi) is simply superb! 🤤 If you love mouth-watering, sweet, and spicy noodles, this is a must-visit spot! #서울맛집 #의정부맛집 #koreanfood #seoul #streetfood

반세기, 50여년 동안 모든 걸 직접 손으로 만드는 의정부 함흥냉면 📍흥미관  (주소: 경기 의정부시 태평로73번길 20 제일시장 다동 63호) 🔗네이버 지도 링크: https://naver.me/xQNb7Lqr 🧊비빔냉면 9천원 ⏰매일 9시~20시 의정부 제일시장에서 50년 넘는 동안 함흥냉면의 자존심을 지켜오고 있는 ‘흥미관’입니다. ​이곳의 냉면이 특별한 이유는 요즘 시대에 정말 보기 드문 ‘수제 반죽’ 방식을 고집하기 때문인데요.  사장님이 직접 반죽하고 바로 뽑아낸 면발은 기성품과는 차원이 다른 쫄깃하고 탱글한 식감을 자랑합니다. ​특히 육수부터 비빔 양념, 고명에 들어가는 모든 재료를 사장님이 직접 정성으로 만드시는데요.  실제로 먹어보니 과하게 달거나 맵거나 짜지 않고, 모든 맛이 조화롭게 어우러진 깊은 ‘손맛’ 그 자체였습니다! ​날이 점점 더워지고 여름이 다가오니 시원하고 쫄깃한 이 집 냉면이 자꾸만 생각나네요. 진정한 노포의 내공이 담긴 냉면 맛집을 찾으신다면 꼭 한 번 방문해 보세요! - 50 Years of Tradition! The Legendary Handmade Cold Noodles 📍 Heungmigwan, Uijeongbu Jeil Market (Address: Da-dong 63, 20 Taepyeong-ro 73beon-gil, Uijeongbu-si, Gyeonggi-do) 🔗 Map: https://naver.me/xQNb7Lqr 🧊 Spicy Mixed Cold Noodles (Bibim-naengmyeon): 9,000 KRW ⏰ Hours: Daily 9:00 AM – 8:00 PM Welcome to 'Heungmigwan,' a place that has proudly preserved the tradition of Hamhung Naengmyeon for over 50 years in Uijeongbu Jeil Market. What makes this place special is its insistence on the "handmade dough" method, which is rare to find these days. The noodles, freshly kneaded and pressed by the owner, boast a chewy and bouncy texture that is on a whole different level from factory-made ones. From the broth to the spicy seasoning and toppings, every ingredient is crafted by hand with great care. The taste is perfectly balanced—not too sweet, spicy, or salty—offering the deep flavor of true craftsmanship. As the days get warmer, you'll find yourself craving these refreshing and chewy noodles. If you're looking for an authentic, time-honored noodle spot, this is it! #서울맛집 #의정부 #koreanfood #seoul #streetfood

This Viral Esports Crying Meme Isn’t AI…Here’s the Truth #gaming #esports #t1 #t1dumbo #gamingnews  The origin of this new esports meme is not what you think, that is if you think it’s AI think again. Many people have been sharing this face and viral gif over the last few hours, but no one seems to have a credible and easy source to find it. It’s also so ridiculous and over the top, it’s hard to imagine that there’s actually happened, but that leaves only one other answer that it’s AI. However, there’s another answer and it’s that it’s a skit featuring a famous YouTuber from Korea done in partnership with T1. This was an April fool’s joke, and it’s now resurfacing on the Internet and going extremely viral. This crying meme of a T1 player losing in esports is probably going to be something you see for the next few years so I wanted to make sure you all knew the origin of it! It was fun research researching this for today’s video, and make sure to share this with your friends if they’ve seen the GIF or shared the gift with you but not known where it came from. If you like gaming insights news and commentary like this, remember to follow the channel.

a hidden cafe in korea with a canoe 🛶 just outside jeonju is this really unique cafe. I arrived midday and it was completely empty! the owners were surprised I spoke english and even asked how I found them. it’s a spacious hanok-style cafe with traditional wooden buildings, views of water fountains, and even a canoe you can sit in with your coffee. trying to steer and film was a bit chaotic but still really fun. the food was honestly just ok but the vibes were 10/10. tips for your visit ~ 🚗 best way to get there is by car or taxi, about 20–30 minutes from jeonju hanok village 🚕 taxis are easy to call on kakao t or uber and relatively affordable 🚌 public transport is possible but takes longer and requires some walking ⏰ typically open 11:00–20:00 and often closed on sundays 💸 drinks are around ₩6,000–₩8,000 per person 📍 cafe tirol 카페 티롤, wanju south korea south korea travel guide | jeonju travel guide | unique cafes in south korea | aesthetic cafes korea | korea travel itinerary #southkoreatravel #koreatrip #travelsouthkorea #jeonju #koreacafe
